findings.explanation: The title says countries of Oceania, but several entries are not countries: Hawaii, Tahiti, and Guam are territories/regions rather than sovereign countries. This makes the puzzle premise misleading for an easy geography puzzle.
findings.suggestedFix: Retitle away from “countries” or replace these with sovereign states/clear island groups.

findings.explanation: “Батик” is primarily associated with Indonesia/Southeast Asia, not a clear Oceania culture item. “Канoe” also has a spelling/foreign-script issue and is too broad as a culture clue.
findings.suggestedFix: Use clearer Oceania culture items and write “каноэ” in Russian.

findings.explanation: Many wind names are specialist geography terms rather than easy everyday vocabulary, especially “Низовка”, “Сарма”, “Бакланий ветер”, “Горний”, “Трамонтана”, “Харматтан”, “Катабатический”, and “Анабатический”.
findings.suggestedFix: Move to a harder tier or replace with common wind/weather terms.

findings.explanation: The category axes overlap: some winds are defined by geography while others by physical mechanism or climate zone. “Бора” is also a katabatic wind, and “бриз” is not specifically a mountain wind.
findings.suggestedFix: Use one organizing principle only, such as regional named winds or mechanism-based wind types.

findings.explanation: “Водные элементы” is a subset of “Элементы сада”, so the category labels overlap conceptually.
findings.suggestedFix: Rename/split so the non-water elements category is clearly distinct, or replace the water category with another garden-type category.

findings.explanation: “Розарий”, “дендрарий”, and “рокарий” are garden types/areas as much as functional or element labels, making their placement less clean.
findings.suggestedFix: Use more concrete functional garden items or make a dedicated “садовые зоны/типы” category.

findings.explanation: Several items are not actual answer-like items but incomplete record prompts, e.g. “Самое долгоживущее” lacks what record holder is meant, while “Синий кит” is a record holder without the same format as the other animal entries.
findings.suggestedFix: Use consistent concrete record holders or consistent record descriptions in every group.

findings.explanation: The star categories mix overlapping taxonomies. “G-звёзды” and “Жёлтый карлик” can describe the same kind of star, and known stars such as the Sun, Sirius, and Betelgeuse also have spectral/size classes.
findings.suggestedFix: Avoid mixing taxonomy labels with individual examples, or make all groups parallel.

findings.explanation: “Переменная” and “Новая звезда” are not types of multiple-star systems; they describe variability/outburst phenomena.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with clean multiple-system terms such as “кратная”, “визуально-двойная”, or retitle the group.

findings.explanation: Leonardo DiCaprio and Denzel Washington are also action stars in common usage, while Jackie Chan, Schwarzenegger, Stallone, and Van Damme are Hollywood actors. The labels overlap heavily.
findings.suggestedFix: Use mutually exclusive labels such as actors by country/era, or make all groups genre-based.

findings.explanation: Гайдай is primarily famous as a film director, not as a Soviet actor category member alongside Nikulin, Smoktunovsky, and Mironov.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with a clearly Soviet actor.

findings.explanation: “Известные”, “Редкие”, and “С сильным запахом” are subjective/descriptive buckets that can overlap with citrus or with one another, rather than mutually exclusive fruit types.
findings.suggestedFix: Use objective categories, e.g. citrus, palm fruits, berries/botanical types, Southeast Asian fruits.

findings.explanation: “Хлебное дерево” is the tree, not the fruit; the fruit is usually “хлебный плод”.
findings.suggestedFix: Use “хлебный плод” or replace it.

findings.explanation: The labels overlap: “Вышибалы” is both a team game and a running/chasing game; “Прятки в воде” overlaps directly with “Прятки”; many Russian folk games are also outdoor/running games.
findings.suggestedFix: Use mutually exclusive settings/equipment or replace overlapping games.

findings.explanation: “Бассейн” is a place, not a game.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with an actual water game.

findings.explanation: “Вавилов” appears twice, once as a physicist surname and once as “Вавилов Ник.” for the biologist. In a surname-only puzzle this creates avoidable ambiguity between Sergei and Nikolai Vavilov.
findings.suggestedFix: Use full names or replace one Vavilov.

findings.explanation: “Космонавтика” includes Гагарин, who is a cosmonaut rather than a scientist; the title “Российские учёные” does not fit him.
findings.suggestedFix: Retitle to include figures of science/cosmonautics or replace Гагарин with a scientist/engineer.

findings.explanation: Some listed “records” are not current or are too vague. Michael Johnson no longer holds the men’s 200 m world record, and “Сифан Хасан 5000 м” is not the current 5000 m world record holder.
findings.suggestedFix: Use stable categories or verify current record holders before publishing.

findings.explanation: The puzzle relies on detailed sports-record knowledge and specific record holders, which is not easy-tier concrete everyday knowledge.
findings.suggestedFix: Move to a harder tier or use simple sport categories without record specifics.

findings.explanation: “Регионарная” overlaps with “Местная анестезия” in common medical taxonomy; проводниковая/плексусная/эпидуральная are related regional techniques. This is too nested for a clean grouping puzzle.
findings.suggestedFix: Use broader non-overlapping categories such as methods, routes, drugs, and monitoring terms.

findings.explanation: Пропофол and кетамин are general anesthetic/sedation agents, not local anesthetics like lidocaine and bupivacaine, so the label “Анестетики” is too broad relative to the other anesthesia-type labels.
findings.suggestedFix: Rename the group “лекарства для анестезии” or split local and general drugs clearly.

findings.explanation: Several items do not match their labels: “Хохлома” and “Гжель” are Russian decorative styles rather than textile ethnic patterns; “Медальон” and “Дамаск” are not specifically floral patterns.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with clear textile pattern names that match each label.

findings.explanation: “Зигзаг зигзаг” appears to be an accidental duplicate/typo rather than a clean item.
findings.suggestedFix: Use “зигзаг” once or replace it.

findings.explanation: The title says “дорожек” in textiles, but the groups are general textile prints/patterns; “дорожки” is likely the wrong word here.
findings.suggestedFix: Retitle as “Типы узоров в текстиле”.

findings.explanation: Мадагаскар and Сахалин are single islands, not archipelagos. Маврикий is usually a single island/country, not a world archipelago item in this context.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with actual archipelagos.

findings.explanation: The geographic labels overlap with political/regional labels: Russian archipelagos can also be in oceans, and “Мадейра/Канарские” are archipelagos in the Atlantic but also political/geographic regions. The Russian group is not parallel to the ocean groups.
findings.suggestedFix: Use all ocean-basin labels or all country/region labels.

findings.explanation: Black is not normally one of the primary colors in painting; standard painterly primary colors are red, yellow, and blue. The group needs a fourth item from another clear concept or should be renamed.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace black or retitle as a different color set.

findings.explanation: The puzzle mixes color names, pigment names, and temperature categories. “Киноварь”, “кобальт”, “ультрамарин”, “охра”, “умбра”, and “сиена” can all be understood as pigments, so labels overlap.
findings.suggestedFix: Use either color-temperature names or pigment categories, not both.

findings.explanation: The song categories overlap by era, authorship, and theme. “День Победы” is a Soviet-era war song but also a named popular song; “Песня о друге” and “Если друг вдруг…” appear to refer to the same Vysotsky song, and author songs can also be lyrical.
findings.suggestedFix: Use mutually exclusive categories such as songs from cartoons, war songs, film songs, and bard songs with non-duplicate titles.

findings.explanation: “Если друг вдруг…” is an opening lyric for “Песня о друге”, so these are effectively the same song listed as two separate items.
findings.suggestedFix: Keep one and replace the other.

findings.explanation: “Калинка” is not specifically a Soviet lyrical song; it is a 19th-century Russian song.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with a clear Soviet lyrical song.

findings.explanation: “Моцарт и Сальери” and “Скупой рыцарь” are individual works within “Маленькие трагедии”, so the dramatic category contains a collection and its components as separate items.
findings.suggestedFix: Use either the collection title or four individual dramatic works, not both.

findings.explanation: The title says aircraft types, but groups mix specific aircraft models, functional military types, light aviation, and helicopters. Helicopters are aircraft too, and “малая авиация” can include small passenger/utility aircraft, so the axes are not fully parallel.
findings.suggestedFix: Use all model categories or all aircraft-type categories.

findings.explanation: “Пар и гриль” is a mixed label and overlaps with “Тепловая обработка”, since steaming, grilling, smoking, and blanching are also heat treatments.
findings.suggestedFix: Make all categories mutually exclusive, e.g. wet heat, dry heat, preservation without heat, modern techniques.

findings.explanation: “Сушка” and “дегидрация” are near-identical processes split across different groups; “мгновенная заморозка” is not a cooking method in the same sense as the other items.
findings.suggestedFix: Avoid splitting synonyms and replace freezing with a cooking/preparation technique.

findings.explanation: The title is “Виды покрытий дорог”, but three of four groups are bridges, tunnels, and interchanges rather than road surfaces. This breaks the puzzle premise.
findings.suggestedFix: Retitle as “Дорожная инфраструктура” or replace the non-surface groups.

findings.explanation: “Трубач” is not an everyday Russian name for an interchange type and may be unclear compared with “труба” or “трубовидная развязка”.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a standard Russian term or a more common interchange type.

findings.explanation: This is specialist medical vocabulary and too technical for an easy puzzle, especially “вентрикулостомия”, “стереотаксия”, “артродез”, “остеотомия”, and “АКШ”.
findings.suggestedFix: Move to a hard/medical tier or use broad everyday surgery categories.

findings.explanation: “Грыжа” is a condition, not the operation; the operation would be hernia repair/hernioplasty.
findings.suggestedFix: Use “герниопластика” or another abdominal operation.

findings.explanation: The title “Стражи и охранники” suggests people/roles, but categories include institutions and processes. “ФСБ”, “Прокуратура”, “Армия”, and “Флот” are organizations, while “паспортный контроль” is a process/location.
findings.suggestedFix: Use either roles only or institution names only.

findings.explanation: “Карантинный” is an adjective, not a clear guard/official role by itself.
findings.suggestedFix: Use “карантинный инспектор” or replace it.

findings.explanation: The title is forest types, but several groups contain tree species rather than forest types. A player could sort “ель”, “кедр”, “пихта”, “дуб”, etc. by tree category instead of forest category.
findings.suggestedFix: Use forest-type names consistently, e.g. ельник, кедрач, дубрава, буковый лес.

findings.explanation: “Пойменный лес” is a riparian/floodplain forest type, not necessarily a mixed forest, so its placement is not uniquely determined.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with a clear mixed-forest example.

findings.explanation: Some roles overlap across construction project management categories: “инженер ПТО”, “мастер участка”, and “сметчик” can be office/project-control roles rather than cleanly separated by design vs management.
findings.suggestedFix: Use clearer role buckets such as design, masonry/carpentry, finishing, site management.

findings.explanation: “Исторические”, “Российские”, and “Катастрофы” are not mutually exclusive: “Аврора”, “Потёмкин”, “Варяг”, “Марат”, “Титаник”, and “Лузитания” are all historical ships, and “Бисмарк” is both historical and a sinking/catastrophe item.
findings.suggestedFix: Use parallel labels such as exploration ships, warships, disaster ships, fictional ships.

findings.explanation: “Арго” is both a mythological/fictional ship and an ancient legendary/historical-culture ship, so it can feel less clean against “Исторические”.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a modern fictional ship that cannot be mistaken for a historical/legendary vessel.

findings.explanation: “Черепаха” is a very common pet and not necessarily exotic, so it can reasonably fit outside the “Экзотические” bucket.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with a more clearly exotic pet or retitle the group.

findings.explanation: “Сумч. летяга” is abbreviated and obscure for easy-tier Russian pet vocabulary.
findings.suggestedFix: Write the full common name or use a more familiar exotic pet.

findings.explanation: “Беспроводные” and “Цифровые” overlap: Wi-Fi, Bluetooth, mobile phones, messengers, video calls, and satellite communication are commonly digital and wireless depending on use.
findings.suggestedFix: Use one axis only, such as historical/personal/broadcast/internet services.

findings.explanation: “Локальная сеть” can be wired or wireless, so placing it under “Проводные” is not uniquely determined.
findings.suggestedFix: Use “проводной Ethernet” or another clearly wired item.

findings.explanation: “Кисломолочные” are also “молочные продукты”, so the labels are nested and overlap.
findings.suggestedFix: Rename the fourth group to a non-fermented product category or split by product type more cleanly.

findings.explanation: “Топлёное молоко” is milk rather than a separate dairy product category member, so it can fit under animal milk or processed milk depending on interpretation.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a clearly non-milk dairy product.

findings.explanation: “Медленный сон” includes deep sleep, so “Глубокий сон” is not a separate peer stage. “Дремота” can also be a sleep stage/transition state.
findings.suggestedFix: Use standard sleep stages or retitle as “состояния сна”.

findings.explanation: “Гипнагогический” is an adjective for the transition into sleep and can refer to hallucinations/images rather than a type of dream; it overlaps conceptually with drowsiness/stages.
findings.suggestedFix: Use “гипнагогические образы” or replace with a common dream type.

findings.explanation: The material categories are not unique because items like “пакет”, “ведро”, “контейнер”, “тубус”, “тюбик”, and “флакон” can be made from different materials. The same generic container type appears under multiple material labels.
findings.suggestedFix: Make every item material-specific, e.g. “пластиковое ведро”, “бумажный тубус”, “металлический тюбик”.

findings.explanation: “Флаконик” is an odd diminutive and less standard than “флакон”.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a standard packaging term.

findings.explanation: “Шуруповёрт” is usually a power tool, which conflicts with the title “ручные инструменты”.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with a clearly manual fastening tool.

findings.explanation: “Пила” and “ножовка” are near-overlapping hand saw terms in the same group, while “рубанок” is primarily a planing/shaping tool rather than a cutting tool in the same sense.
findings.suggestedFix: Use four distinct cutting hand tools or retitle as woodworking tools.

findings.explanation: “Polo on bicycle” is left in English in an otherwise Russian puzzle and is awkward for the target language.
findings.suggestedFix: Use “велополо”.

findings.explanation: “Слэклайн” and “фридайвинг” are also commonly treated as extreme/nontraditional sports, so the “Экстремальные” and “Нишевые” labels are subjective and overlapping.
findings.suggestedFix: Use objective categories such as board/water/mind/esports.

findings.explanation: The labels overlap strongly: “земляника” and “малина” can be wild/forest berries, “морошка”, “костяника”, “черёмуха”, “калина”, “рябина”, and “барбарис” can all be wild or medicinal. The categories are not mutually exclusive.
findings.suggestedFix: Use one axis such as habitat, color, plant family, or culinary use.

findings.explanation: “Города-герои” and “Ещё города-герои” are the same category split into two groups, which is not a single-concept distinct label set.
findings.suggestedFix: Merge or replace one with a different, objective category.

findings.explanation: “Осада Сталинграда” is not the usual term for the Battle of Stalingrad and does not fit as cleanly as “Блокада Ленинграда” or “Оборона Москвы”.
findings.suggestedFix: Use “Сталинградская битва” with a retitled group, or replace with a true siege/blockade.

findings.explanation: The “Культура” group mixes objects/monuments tied to the peoples in the regional groups: Chichen Itza is Maya, Machu Picchu is Inca, and wigwams/totem poles are associated with North American Indigenous cultures. This creates semantic overlap with the people groups.
findings.suggestedFix: Use four parallel regional people categories or four parallel cultural-object categories.

findings.explanation: Culinary, medicinal, tea, and garden-pr spice categories overlap heavily: mint, thyme/chabrets, oregano/dushitsa, rosemary, sage, lavender, basil, melissa, and chamomile can fit multiple uses.
findings.suggestedFix: Use objective botanical or culinary categories that do not overlap.

findings.explanation: “Чабрец” and “тимьян” are commonly treated as the same herb/name pair in Russian usage, split across two groups.
findings.suggestedFix: Keep one and replace the other.

findings.explanation: “Питьевая вода” overlaps with “Природные воды” and “Состояния воды”: freshwater can be drinking water, mineral water is drinking water, and snow/ice are natural states/forms of water.
findings.suggestedFix: Use one classification axis or replace drinking-water items with a non-overlapping group.

findings.explanation: “Карачинская” is less universally known than the other mineral water brands and may be regional for easy tier.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a widely recognized mineral water brand or generic mineral-water types.

findings.explanation: “Мисо-суп” is a dish made with miso, not itself a primary fermented drink; placing it under “Напитки” is wrong.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with a fermented drink.

findings.explanation: “Пахта” is a dairy product/byproduct and can overlap with the milk-fermentation group; “кимчи” and “кимчи из редьки” are near-duplicate category variants.
findings.suggestedFix: Keep groups parallel and avoid listing one kimchi variant as a separate peer unless the whole group is variants.

findings.explanation: The craft-material categories overlap: “аппликация” and “декупаж” can be paper crafts, while “папье-маше” is paper-based but placed under sculpting.
findings.suggestedFix: Use more precise labels such as textile crafts, yarn crafts, paper crafts, and modeling materials.

findings.explanation: Several idioms are assigned to the wrong trait: “Дерёт три шкуры” means overcharges/exploits rather than simple stinginess; “Гусь лапчатый” means a sly/dubious person, not boasting; “Берёт от потолка” means invents figures, not boasting.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with idioms that directly express the target traits.

findings.explanation: “Рубаха-парень” means an open, friendly person and can relate more to sociability than generosity specifically.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a clearer generosity idiom.

findings.explanation: Several dishes do not match their cooking-method categories. Хинкали are boiled, not steamed in the usual Russian/Caucasus culinary understanding; рассольник and кислые щи are soups, not pickling/salting products; форшмак is not a studen/aspic dish; цыплёнок табака is pan-fried/pressed, not cooked on an open fire.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with dishes that directly match each cooking method.

findings.explanation: The title “Советская кухня” is not well matched to categories that are mostly Caucasian/Central Asian or general dishes rather than specifically Soviet cuisine.
findings.suggestedFix: Retitle as “Кухни народов СССР” or use more recognizably Soviet everyday dishes.

findings.explanation: “Лезет из кожи вон” means tries very hard, not anger.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace with a clear anger idiom.

findings.explanation: “Уши до ушей” is likely a malformed expression; the common phrase is “улыбка до ушей”.
findings.suggestedFix: Use “улыбка до ушей”.

findings.explanation: “Они сражались за неё” is not the title of the Soviet war drama; the film is “Они сражались за Родину”.
findings.suggestedFix: Correct the title.

findings.explanation: “Немое кино 1920-х” is an era/format label, while the other groups are genres. This is not parallel, and some silent films are also dramas/historical films.
findings.suggestedFix: Use all genre labels or all era labels.

findings.explanation: Several idioms are assigned imprecisely or incorrectly: “Ни рыба ни мясо” means neither one thing nor another, not laziness; “Горит работа” means work is going well, not necessarily diligence; “На все руки от скуки” is malformed and should be “на все руки мастер”.
findings.suggestedFix: Replace or correct these idioms.

findings.explanation: “Грызёт науку” is about studying hard, which can indicate diligence, but it is a narrower student/learning idiom and may not align cleanly with general “усердие”.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a broader diligence idiom.

findings.explanation: Many idioms do not match the literal natural-stichia categories. “Как две капли воды” means similarity, not drought; “Ветер в голове” means frivolity, not storm; “Дышит в затылок” means close behind, not frost; “Берёт за душу” means emotionally affects, not frost.
findings.suggestedFix: Use idioms whose meaning or imagery clearly matches each category.

findings.explanation: The categories are literal natural phenomena, but many answers are figurative idioms about emotions/body states; players may sort by words appearing in the idiom rather than meaning, making the intended logic unstable.
findings.suggestedFix: Decide whether grouping is by literal image words or idiom meaning and make all items follow that rule.

findings.explanation: Several items are near-duplicates within a group rather than distinct discoveries: “Периодическая система” and “Закон периодичности” are closely tied; “Беспроводной телеграф” and “Передача без провода” are near-duplicate descriptions.
findings.suggestedFix: Use distinct contributions or retitle as associated terms rather than discoveries.

findings.explanation: “Атомный вес в основе” is an awkward description, not a created discovery item, and “первая антенна” is too broad/contestable as something Popov invented.
findings.suggestedFix: Use standard named contributions.

findings.explanation: “Костёр у озера” can be associated with general camping rather than specifically a pioneer camp, and “Финский сервиз” is an example of deficit/consumer goods rather than the phenomenon itself.
findings.suggestedFix: Use more canonical Soviet pioneer-camp and deficit items.

findings.explanation: Several idioms are malformed or assigned incorrectly: “На словах Толстой” is not a standard honesty/hypocrisy idiom as written; “Слово не с делом” is not honesty; “На чужом горбу в рай” is opportunism, not envy; “Теши — не своротишь” is malformed/unclear.
findings.suggestedFix: Use standard idioms and verify their meanings.

findings.explanation: “Мёд на устах” can mean sweet-talking/flattery rather than hypocrisy specifically; it may be interpreted as deceit or politeness depending on context.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a clearer hypocrisy idiom.

findings.explanation: The title says Peredvizhniki painting, but Levitan and Vasnetsov are not normally grouped as core Peredvizhniki in the same clean way as Repin/Surikov, and the categories are really “artist wrote/painted these works” rather than the movement. Also “Вечерний звон” is not a painting title by Levitan in the expected canon; Levitan’s well-known work is “Вечерний звон” only if referring to a landscape, but it is less canonical than the others for easy tier.
findings.suggestedFix: Retitle as “Русская живопись: кто написал” or use only confirmed Peredvizhniki and very canonical works.

findings.explanation: Matching four painters to four specific works each is not easy-tier unless all works are extremely canonical; several are specialist art-history items.
findings.suggestedFix: Move to medium or simplify to more widely known works.

findings.explanation: “Рука руку моет” can describe corruption/mutual cover-up broadly, not only nepotism, and can overlap with bureaucracy/corruption themes.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a clearer nepotism phrase.

findings.explanation: “По рекомендации” can be a legitimate referral rather than nepotism, so it is somewhat context-dependent.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a phrase with stronger cronyism meaning.

findings.explanation: “Вишнёвый сад Раневской” is awkwardly phrased as if the play is the character; the character is Любовь Раневская. “Дядя Ваня” is both a play title and a character nickname, so it is less clean as a character item.
findings.suggestedFix: Use clear character names such as Раневская and Астров/Треплев/Лопахин.

findings.explanation: “Герасим” can refer to multiple Russian literary characters; without a patronymic/title, players may not immediately associate him with Tolstoy’s “После бала”/other Tolstoy context. It is less unambiguous than the other Tolstoy names.
findings.suggestedFix: Use a more canonical Tolstoy character or disambiguate with the work.
